verdict.summary: The prior major finding is resolved. Both the post-gate token mint and follow-up creation now use an explicit always() status function while retaining the model-success and non-dry-run guards, so they remain eligible after a failed gate. Publishing remains fail-closed on gate success. The added self-test is non-vacuous: it checks the two positive always() requirements, the mint's model-success guard, and uses the PR step as a negative control while separately confirming its gate-success condition. No new correctness, soundness, or security issue is evident in the diff.
